meta.title: Quantum_Shield — Documentation Generation Handoff
meta.purpose: Complete knowledge base for generating ALL enterprise documentation. Feed this entire file to Claude and ask it to generate each document listed in the 'documents_to_generate' section.
meta.generated_by: Claude Opus 4.6 — after completing Phases 1-5 of Enterprise development
meta.instructions_for_receiving_claude: Use this JSON as the single source of truth. Generate each document in the 'documents_to_generate' array. Each document spec includes its title, audience, format, and complete content outline. Do NOT invent features — only document what is listed as 'status: COMPLETE' in this file.

product_identity.name: Quantum_Shield
product_identity.tagline: Portable Encrypted File Storage — Intelligence-Grade Security for Everyone
product_identity.mission: Carry sensitive files in your pocket, plug into any computer running Windows, macOS, or Linux, access your files with a password, and walk away leaving zero evidence you were ever there.
product_identity.company_promise: USBVault was engineered to the operational standards demanded by intelligence professionals — and made accessible to everyone who needs that level of protection.

PORTABLE.description: No installation. Runs from USB via double-click launcher. Portable Node.js bundled on TOOLS partition.

SECURE.description: Argon2id (64 MiB) + XChaCha20-Poly1305 / AES-256-GCM-SIV. Rust crypto core with constant-time comparisons.

INVISIBLE.description: SECURE partition unmounted (invisible). chflags hidden (macOS), attrib +H+S (Windows). Encrypted filenames in AEAD chunks.

RESILIENT.description: Dual-index atomic commits. Monotonic commit counter. Append-only VAULT.bin. State version rollback protection.

SIMPLE.description: Double-click launcher → browser opens → password → done. No technical knowledge required.

ZERO_TRACE.description: 23 forensic artifact cleaners across 3 platforms. Auto-clean on eject. Restart advisory displayed.

ZERO_TRUST.description: Wrapped MEK architecture (password→KEK→MEK). Cloud split-key (HKDF(LOCAL||REMOTE)) planned for V3.0.

PQC_COMPLIANT.description: ML-KEM-1024 + X25519 hybrid sealed boxes. Feature-gated in Rust. Quantum-resistant key exchange.

architecture.overview: Four-subsystem architecture with zero-knowledge design. All crypto runs client-side in Rust. Server never sees plaintext. Dual-mode: USB-only (offline) or cloud-connected.

subsystems.name: usbvault-crypto
subsystems.purpose: ALL cryptographic operations — Argon2id KDF, AEAD ciphers, streaming encryption, vault header, PQC hybrid KEM, TFA credential packing, memory security

subsystems.name: usbvault-app
subsystems.purpose: Cross-platform frontend — web, iOS, Android. File management, vault operations, settings, educational content
subsystems.state_management: Zustand (7 stores: auth, vault, theme, sidebar, language, offline, sync)

dual_mode.cloud_connected: EXPO_PUBLIC_API_URL set → SRP-6a auth → cloud sync/sharing/billing
dual_mode.usb_standalone: No API URL → vault password is session → sessionStorage only → zero-trace

subsystems.name: usbvault-server
subsystems.purpose: REST API server — authentication, vault management, blob storage, billing, sync
subsystems.zero_knowledge: Server NEVER handles plaintext, filenames, or encryption keys

subsystems.name: usb-companion
subsystems.purpose: Local USB hardware bridge — runs on user's machine, bridges app to OS USB subsystem
subsystems.standalone_mode: Also serves static web app (express.static) when deployed to USB TOOLS partition

key_architecture_decisions.title: PQC Hybrid vs V3 fixed header block
Zustand reactive stores vs Qt signals
Build pipeline vs Ed25519 manifest signing

streaming.format: magic(4B) + version(1B) + base_nonce(24B) + chunks[length(4B) + AEAD(data + 16B tag)] + final_HMAC(32B)

key_architecture.benefit: Password change is O(1) — only re-wraps MEK
key_architecture.fido2: If enabled: final_key = enc_key XOR PRF_output

integrity.header_hmac: HMAC-SHA256 over all header fields (HMAC field zeroed during compute)
integrity.verify_marker: Encrypt 'USBVAULT_VERIFY_OK_0000' with MEK — decrypt to confirm password

self_destruct.action: 3-pass overwrite of wrapped_mek: random → zeros → random
self_destruct.result: Vault permanently inaccessible — data records remain but can't be decrypted

backoff.enforcement: vaultOrchestrator.ts blocks unlock attempts during cooldown

pqc.security: Secure if EITHER X25519 OR ML-KEM-1024 remains unbroken

vault_bin.fifty_percent_rule: VAULT.bin never exceeds 50% of SECURE partition

vault_format.tfa_wire_format: cred_id_len(2B u16 LE) + credential_id(var) + aaguid(16B) + label_len(1B) + label(var, max 32B)

defense_in_depth.name: Hardware Key (FIDO2)
defense_in_depth.description: PRF/hmac-secret extension, XOR with Argon2id key

password_policy.weak_dictionary: 98,735 entries via SHA-256 bloom filter (k=10, FPR ~0.1%)
password_policy.hibp: k-anonymity API with bloom filter fallback
password_policy.app_password: Separate gate: PBKDF2-SHA256, 150K iterations, 12-char min, 3 attempts, 30s lockout

first_time_setup: Double-click launcher (or run from dev server)
Login / register (or USB standalone: just enter email + password)
Onboarding wizard: PQC detection → cipher selection → identity → confirm
Select USB drive, enter vault name and master password
System creates dual partitions (TOOLS + SECURE), writes VAULT.bin header
daily_use: Double-click launcher from TOOLS partition (or use dev server)
Enter password (exponential backoff if failed attempts)
If FIDO2 enabled: tap hardware key (PRF extension)
Dashboard shows vault files and security status
Add files: pick file → vaultOrchestrator encrypts (Rust FFI) → appends to VAULT.bin → atomic index commit
Decrypt files: select → vaultOrchestrator reads + decrypts → download or temp view
Remove files: select → remove from index → atomic commit (data orphaned, reclaimed by compact)
Compact vault: rewrites VAULT.bin with only active records
Eject: zero-trace cleanup (23 artifacts) → unmount SECURE → eject drive → restart advisory

recovery.forgotten_password: 24-word BIP39 recovery phrase (shown once at provisioning, user must save)
recovery.lost_fido2_key: Recovery blob (AES-GCM-SIV encrypted) stored in vault header
recovery.corrupted_vault: Dual-index fallback — if active index corrupted, use backup slot
recovery.usb_failure: Cloud backup restore (if cloud-connected mode used)

go.coverage: Auth, billing, sharing, sync, middleware, BOLA

Your Master Password (why 15+ characters, password tips, NEVER share, NEVER forget, recovery phrase)
Daily Use (plug in → double-click → password → you're in)
Adding Files (select file → choose encryption → one click → file encrypted and stored)
Viewing Files (select file → decrypt → view or download)
Removing Files (select → delete → confirm → compaction available)
Safely Ejecting (click Eject → zero-trace cleanup runs → restart advisory → remove USB)

Settings (theme, language, security, account, notifications)

documents_to_generate.title: Quantum_Shield — Recovery Procedures
sections: Recovery Phrase (what it is, why you need it, how to use it, what happens if lost)
Forgotten Master Password (use recovery phrase → create new vault → import backup)
Lost FIDO2 Hardware Key (recovery blob in vault header, AES-GCM-SIV encrypted backup key)
Corrupted Vault (dual-index recovery — if active slot corrupted, fall back to backup slot)
USB Drive Failure (cloud backup restore if cloud-connected, otherwise data is lost — importance of backups)
Self-Destruct Triggered (vault is permanently destroyed — this is by design, restore from backup only)
Wrong Password Lockout (exponential backoff — wait up to 1 hour, or use different session)
Vault Not Detected (check SECURE partition mounted, verify VAULT.bin exists, try manual mount)
Companion Service Won't Start (port 3001 conflict, Node.js version, permission issues)
Cross-Platform Issues (vault created on Windows, opened on macOS — should work, ExFAT is universal)
